meta.name: DAR-5 Dimensions of Anger Reactions
meta.description: The DAR-5 is a 5-item ultra-brief scale that rapidly assesses anger problems across five dimensions: frequency, intensity, duration, aggression, and interference.
meta.author: Forbes et al. (2014)
meta.reference: Forbes D, et al. A new measure of anger: The DAR-5. Journal of Clinical Psychology. 2014;70(8):781-789.
meta.instruction: Over the last 4 weeks, to what extent have you experienced the following?

questions.text: I often find myself getting angry at people or situations
When I get angry, I get really mad
When I get angry, I stay angry
When I get angry at someone, I want to hit or clobber the person
My anger prevents me from getting along with people as well as I'd like to

ranges.label: Normal
ranges.description: Your anger reactions are within the normal range. You manage frustration and anger effectively, without significant impairment in your daily functioning or relationships. Maintaining healthy anger management skills is beneficial for long-term well-being.
suggestions: Continue using effective anger management strategies
Practice stress reduction techniques to prevent anger buildup
Maintain open communication in your relationships about frustrations

ranges.label: Mild
ranges.description: You experience mildly elevated anger reactions. While not severely problematic, you may notice occasional difficulty controlling anger intensity or duration. Learning additional anger management techniques can help prevent escalation.
suggestions: Practice the ‘pause and breathe’ technique — take slow deep breaths before responding when angry
Identify your anger triggers and develop specific coping plans for each
Use physical activity as a healthy outlet for frustration and tension
Try cognitive reappraisal — reframe situations to reduce anger intensity

ranges.label: Moderate
ranges.description: Your anger reactions are moderately elevated, suggesting notable difficulty with anger intensity, duration, or expression. This level of anger may be affecting your relationships, work performance, or physical health through chronic stress activation.
suggestions: Consider anger management counseling or a structured anger management program
Practice progressive muscle relaxation to reduce physiological arousal associated with anger
Keep an anger diary to identify patterns, triggers, and early warning signs
Learn assertive communication skills to express needs without aggression
Explore whether underlying issues like stress, depression, or past trauma contribute to anger

ranges.label: Severe
ranges.description: Your anger reactions are significantly elevated, indicating serious difficulty managing anger that is likely impacting your relationships, work, and overall quality of life. Professional intervention is strongly recommended to develop healthier anger regulation strategies.
suggestions: Seek professional help from a therapist specializing in anger management or emotion regulation
Consider cognitive-behavioral therapy (CBT) which has strong evidence for treating anger problems
Develop a safety plan for managing intense anger episodes, including removing yourself from triggering situations
Explore whether medication consultation may be helpful, particularly if anger co-occurs with depression or anxiety
